# terrible_f5networks_f5_modules_bigip_monitor_snmp_dca (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against
- `name` (String) Monitor name.

### Optional

- `agent_type` (String) Specifies the SNMP agent running on the monitored server. When creating a new monitor, the default is `UCD` (UC-Davis).
- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `community` (String) Specifies the community name the system must use to authenticate with the host server through SNMP. When creating a new monitor, the default value is `public`. This value is case sensitive.
- `cpu_coefficient` (String) Specifies the coefficient the system uses to calculate the weight of the CPU threshold in the dynamic ratio load balancing algorithm. When creating a new monitor, the default is `1.5`.
- `cpu_threshold` (Number) Specifies the maximum acceptable CPU usage on the target server. When creating a new monitor, the default is `80` percent.
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `description` (String) Specifies descriptive text that identifies the monitor.
- `disk_coefficient` (String) Specifies the coefficient the system uses to calculate the weight of the disk threshold in the dynamic ratio load balancing algorithm. When creating a new monitor, the default is `2.0`.
- `disk_threshold` (Number) Specifies the maximum acceptable disk usage on the target server. When creating a new monitor, the default is `90` percent.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of name→value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `interval` (Number) Specifies the frequency, in seconds, at which the system issues the monitor check when either the resource is down or the status of the resource is unknown. When creating a new monitor, the default is `10`.
- `memory_coefficient` (String) Specifies the coefficient the system uses to calculate the weight of the memory threshold in the dynamic ratio load balancing algorithm. When creating a new monitor, the default is `1.0`.
- `memory_threshold` (Number) Specifies the maximum acceptable memory usage on the target server. When creating a new monitor, the default is `70` percent.
- `parent` (String) The parent template of this monitor template. Once this value has been set, it cannot be changed. By default, this value is the `snmp_dca` parent on the `Common` partition.
- `partition` (String) Device partition to manage resources on.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `state` (String) When `present`, ensures the monitor exists. When `absent`, ensures the monitor is removed.
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `time_until_up` (Number) Specifies the number of seconds to wait after a resource first responds correctly to the monitor before setting the resource to 'up'. During the interval, all responses from the resource must be correct. When the interval expires, the resource is marked 'up'. A value of 0, means that the resource is marked up immediately upon receipt of the first correct response. When creating a new monitor, the default is `0`.
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution
- `version` (String) Specifies the version of SNMP the host server uses. When creating a new monitor, the default is `v1`. When `v1`, specifies the host server uses SNMP version 1. When `v2c`, specifies that the host server uses SNMP version 2c.

### Read-Only

- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
